geographic.title: Proximity to National Laboratories
geographic.description: Located within 250 miles of Sandia National Laboratories, Los Alamos National Laboratory, and White Sands Missile Range, enabling close collaboration and facility access.

geographic.title: US-Mexico Border Research Corridor
geographic.description: Unique position on the US-Mexico border provides access to binational research networks, cross-border data systems, and international collaboration pipelines relevant to trade, logistics, and environmental research.

geographic.title: Southwest Energy and Water Hub
geographic.description: Situated in a region with major solar, wind, and natural gas resources, plus critical water scarcity challenges. Direct access to real-world energy and water infrastructure for field research.

geographic.title: Fort Bliss and Defense Ecosystem
geographic.description: Adjacent to one of the largest military installations in the US, providing proximity to defense research applications, testing ranges, and Army Research Laboratory personnel.

institutional.title: Carnegie R1 Research University
institutional.description: Classified as a Doctoral University with Very High Research Activity, reflecting sustained research output and doctoral program strength across engineering and sciences.

institutional.title: Institute for Applied AI Innovation (AAII)
institutional.description: Founded in 2025 under UTEP Research & Innovation, AAII provides cross-disciplinary AI research infrastructure, GPU computing resources (NVIDIA H100 DGX), and collaborative workspace in the Interdisciplinary Research Building.

institutional.title: Aerospace Center
institutional.description: Nationally recognized research hub advancing aerospace technologies in propulsion, space exploration, and advanced manufacturing. Partners with NASA, the Department of Defense, and industry.

institutional.title: Established DOE Research Portfolio
institutional.description: Track record of DOE-funded research across multiple departments, demonstrating institutional capacity to manage federal research programs.

institutional.title: Hispanic-Serving Institution (HSI)
institutional.description: As a leading HSI with a majority Hispanic student body, UTEP brings unique workforce diversity and cultural competence to research teams, aligning with DOE priorities for broadening participation.

infrastructure.title: High-Performance Computing and Cyberinfrastructure
infrastructure.description: GPU resources through NVIDIA H100 DGX and HGX systems, access to UT System's Texas Advanced Computing Center (TACC), and Cyber-SHARE data-sharing infrastructure for collaborative research.

infrastructure.title: W.M. Keck Center for 3-D Innovation
infrastructure.description: Leading research facility in additive manufacturing and 3D printing, developing advanced technologies for aerospace, biomedical, and defense applications.

infrastructure.title: Center for Inland Desalination Systems (CIDS)
infrastructure.description: Develops innovative technologies to treat and reuse brackish groundwater, wastewater, and nontraditional water sources, expanding sustainable water supplies for arid regions.

infrastructure.title: Research Institute for Manufacturing & Engineering Systems
infrastructure.description: Advances research in automation, robotics, and advanced manufacturing processes with industry and government partners.

infrastructure.title: Border Biomedical Research Center (BBRC)
infrastructure.description: NIH-funded center supporting interdisciplinary research on health disparities and diseases affecting Hispanic and border populations, with advanced core facilities.

infrastructure.title: Visualization Wall and Scientific Computing
infrastructure.description: Ultra-high resolution display surface for Scientific Exploratory Research and Collaboration, supporting complex data visualization across disciplines.
